products.name: Logitech G Pro X Superlight 2
products.verdict: The gold standard for competitive FPS gaming — featherlight at 60g with flawless tracking.
products.description: The Logitech G Pro X Superlight 2 is the go-to mouse for esports professionals worldwide. Weighing just 60 grams, it pairs the HERO 2 sensor with LIGHTFORCE hybrid switches for zero-compromise speed and precision. The refined shape, extended battery life up to 95 hours, and LIGHTSPEED wireless ensure tournament-grade reliability without the cable.
pros: Universally safe shape for most grip styles
cons: Premium price for a simple design
No RGB lighting
Only one scroll wheel mode (no infinite scroll)

products.name: Razer DeathAdder V3 HyperSpeed
products.verdict: The best ergonomic gaming mouse — classic DeathAdder shape perfected for wireless play.
products.description: The Razer DeathAdder V3 HyperSpeed brings the iconic ergonomic shape into the modern wireless era at a more accessible price. The Focus Pro 36K sensor delivers flawless tracking on any surface, while the ultra-lightweight 55g body and HyperSpeed wireless keep you competitive. Up to 235 hours on a single AA battery makes charging worries a thing of the past.
pros: Legendary ergonomic comfort for palm grip
Incredible battery life on a single AA
cons: Right-hand only — no left-hand option
AA battery adds slight weight vs rechargeable
No RGB lighting

products.name: Razer Viper V3 Pro
products.verdict: Razer's lightest and fastest wireless mouse ever — built for esports dominance.
products.description: The Razer Viper V3 Pro pushes the boundaries of competitive gaming mice with a stunning 54g weight and the Focus Pro 36K sensor. Its symmetrical ambidextrous shape works for claw and fingertip grips, while the new Gen-3 optical switches deliver zero-bounce actuation. With 95 hours of wireless battery life and 8000Hz HyperPolling support, this is Razer's flagship without compromise.
pros: 54g is absurdly light for a full-featured mouse
8000Hz polling for the smoothest tracking possible
Excellent build quality despite low weight
Works for both claw and fingertip grips
cons: Very expensive at $159
Ambidextrous shape may not suit palm grip
Side buttons only on left side

products.name: Logitech G502 X Plus
products.verdict: The feature-packed powerhouse — customizable everything with a premium wireless experience.
products.description: The G502 X Plus is the wireless evolution of Logitech's most popular gaming mouse ever. It packs the HERO 25K sensor, LIGHTFORCE hybrid switches, a reversible DPI-shift button, and a free-spinning scroll wheel into a sculpted ergonomic body. Full RGB LIGHTSYNC and up to 130 hours of battery life round out a mouse that does everything well.
cons: Heavy at 106g compared to modern ultralights
Complex shape isn't for everyone
Expensive for a non-ultralight mouse

products.name: SteelSeries Aerox 5 Wireless
products.verdict: A versatile multi-genre mouse with extra buttons, light weight, and solid wireless performance.
products.description: The SteelSeries Aerox 5 Wireless bridges the gap between ultralight FPS mice and button-heavy MMO mice. At 74g with 9 programmable buttons (including a side rocker switch), it handles everything from shooters to strategy games. The TrueMove Air sensor, dual wireless connectivity, and IP54 water resistance make it a practical all-rounder for gamers who play across genres.
pros: Great button count without heavy weight
IP54 rating is rare for gaming mice
Works well across multiple game genres
cons: Side rocker switch takes getting used to
Honeycomb holes collect dust over time
Sensor not as cutting-edge as competitors

products.name: Razer Basilisk V3
products.verdict: The best wired gaming mouse for the money — feature-loaded with a smart scroll wheel.
products.description: The Razer Basilisk V3 delivers a premium wired experience at a mid-range price. Its Focus Pro 26K sensor, HyperScroll tilt wheel with smart-shift, and 11 programmable buttons make it one of the most versatile wired mice available. Razer Chroma RGB adds flair, while the ergonomic thumb rest keeps long sessions comfortable.
pros: Smart scroll wheel is a game-changer
cons: Wired only — no wireless option
101g is heavy by modern standards
Scroll wheel can be noisy in ratchet mode

products.name: Logitech G305 Lightspeed
products.verdict: The unbeatable budget wireless gaming mouse — LIGHTSPEED tech at half the price.
products.description: The Logitech G305 proves you don't need to spend $150 for wireless gaming. Powered by the HERO sensor and Logitech's LIGHTSPEED wireless technology, it delivers lag-free performance indistinguishable from wired mice. A single AA battery lasts up to 250 hours, and the compact shape suits claw and fingertip grips perfectly. It's the best value in wireless gaming mice, period.
pros: Unbeatable price for wireless gaming performance
cons: Sensor maxes at 12K DPI (fine for most, not pros)
AA battery adds weight
Basic build — no rubber grips or premium materials

products.name: Corsair M75 Air Wireless
products.verdict: Corsair's ultralight contender — 60g with top-tier wireless and a comfortable ergonomic shape.
products.description: The Corsair M75 Air Wireless enters the ultralight arena with a 60g ergonomic body, the Marksman 26K sensor, and Corsair's SLIPSTREAM wireless technology. Its comfortable right-hand shape sets it apart from the ambidextrous ultralights, making it ideal for palm and relaxed claw grip users who want minimal weight without sacrificing comfort. Up to 100 hours of battery life seals the deal.
pros: 60g in a comfortable ergonomic shape is rare
Great for palm grip users wanting ultralight
cons: No USB-C dongle storage in mouse
Side grips could be grippier
iCUE software can be bloated

products.name: Pulsar X2V2 Mini
products.verdict: The claw grip king — ultra-refined symmetrical shape at just 52g for precision gaming.
products.description: The Pulsar X2V2 Mini is a favorite among competitive FPS players who prefer claw grip. At just 52g with the PixArt PAW3395 sensor, it offers raw precision in a clean symmetrical shell with zero holes. The shape is carefully contoured for aggressive claw and fingertip control, and the build quality punches well above its price. Dual wireless modes and 70 hours of battery round it out.
pros: 52g with a solid shell — no honeycomb holes
Perfect shape for claw grip players
cons: Too small for large hands or palm grip
Side buttons could be clickier
70-hour battery is decent but not class-leading

products.name: Glorious Model O 2 Wireless
products.verdict: A polished ultralight wireless mouse at an aggressive price — great for fingertip and claw grip.
products.description: The Glorious Model O 2 Wireless refines the original Model O formula with a solid shell (no holes), the BAMF 2.0 sensor, and Glorious' improved wireless technology. At 68g with a low-profile ambidextrous shape, it's excellent for fingertip and claw grip players. The aggressive $79 price undercuts most competitors while delivering comparable performance and a premium feel.
pros: Low-profile shape great for fingertip grip
Good battery life for the weight
cons: Side buttons are a bit mushy
Scroll wheel steps feel light
Less refined than Logitech/Razer flagships
